stories.title: Iran vows to destroy regional infrastructure if Trump strikes its power plants
stories.description: Tehran warned that energy and desalination facilities would become "legitimate targets" after Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Senate advances Mullin's nomination for DHS secretary
stories.description: The 54-37 vote clears a key procedural hurdle, setting up a final confirmation vote as early as Monday.

stories.title: Zelenskyy demands tighter Russia sanctions as Florida peace talks enter day two
stories.description: Ukraine's president cited 1,550 drone strikes in one week, saying eased oil sanctions fund Russia's war as bilateral talks with the U.S. continue.

stories.title: Trump draws bipartisan rebuke for celebrating Mueller's death
stories.description: Robert Mueller, the former FBI director and special counsel in the Trump-Russia probe, died Friday at 81. Trump said he was "glad" on Truth Social.

stories.title: Houthis could enter Iran war as soon as Monday
stories.description: Saudi Arabia is working to deter the group as it reinforces positions near a key Red Sea port, threatening a second maritime front.

stories.title: Bessent says US has 'plenty of money' for Iran war
stories.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent ruled out tax increases to fund the conflict and defended the Pentagon's request for $200 billion in supplemental funding.

stories.title: Trump threatens to deploy ICE agents to airports Monday amid DHS shutdown
stories.description: The president said immigration officers would provide security and arrest undocumented immigrants as TSA staffing shortages worsen nationwide.

stories.title: IAEA says no radiation leaks after strikes near nuclear sites in Iran, Israel
stories.description: Tit-for-tat attacks hit near Natanz and Dimona on day 23 of the war, with IAEA chief Grossi urging "maximum military restraint."

stories.title: Senate blocks transgender sports ban amendment to voting bill
stories.description: The 49-41 party-line vote rejected a GOP push to attach the ban to the SAVE America Act during a rare weekend session.

stories.title: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in March as US oil blockade tightens
stories.description: The grid collapse left millions without power as aging infrastructure buckles under fuel shortages caused by the Trump administration's economic pressure campaign.

stories.title: US envoy calls Miami talks with Ukraine 'constructive' amid deadly attacks
stories.description: Special Envoy Steve Witkoff said discussions with Ukrainian officials focused on narrowing gaps toward a peace deal as both sides traded fire Saturday.

stories.title: U.S. intensifies strikes to reopen Strait of Hormuz as Iran war roils global energy markets
stories.description: Oil prices have roughly doubled and gas costs jumped since Iran blockaded the strait three weeks ago, stranding thousands of vessels.

stories.title: Rising gas prices are wiping out Trump tax refunds, economists warn
stories.description: Stanford economists found the typical household will spend $740 more on gas this year, nearly matching the $748 boost in tax refunds from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act.

stories.title: G7 demands Iran halt attacks, vows to protect energy supplies
stories.description: The joint statement condemned strikes across eight Middle Eastern nations and backed the right of targeted countries to defend themselves.

stories.title: US says it struck 8,000 Iranian targets in three weeks of war
stories.description: CENTCOM says 130 Iranian vessels have been destroyed, calling it the largest elimination of a navy since World War II.

stories.title: Robert Mueller, former FBI director and special counsel, dies at 81
stories.description: Mueller reshaped the FBI after 9/11 and led the investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 election during Trump's first term.

stories.title: US quietly lifts sanctions on Russian nationals in fourth wave this month
stories.description: The Treasury Department removed two Russian citizens and several other entities from its sanctions list on March 20, offering no explanation.

stories.title: UK expands US base access to strike Iran's Strait of Hormuz missile sites
stories.description: Starmer had initially limited US use of British bases to defensive measures but broadened the terms Friday to include protecting commercial shipping.

stories.title: Indian, Asian refiners move to buy Iranian oil after US sanctions waiver
stories.description: The 30-day exemption covers roughly 170 million barrels of Iranian crude already at sea, the third such waiver in two weeks.

stories.title: Gold suffers worst weekly drop since 1983 as war stokes rate fears
stories.description: The metal shed roughly 10% in a week, defying its safe-haven reputation as the Iran conflict rewired inflation and interest rate expectations.

stories.title: Democrats conduct informal vote counts on ousting Schumer
stories.description: Sen. Chris Murphy revealed that Democratic lawmakers have tallied votes on removing Schumer as minority leader, though he said Schumer retains enough support to stay.

stories.title: Trump lifts sanctions on Iranian oil at sea to ease surging prices
stories.description: The Treasury Department issued a general license Friday permitting global sale of roughly 140 million barrels of Iranian crude through April 19.

stories.title: Switzerland halts new arms exports to US over Iran war
stories.description: Bern cited its neutrality obligations, saying no new weapons licenses have been issued to Washington since the conflict began Feb. 28.

stories.title: Judge blocks Pentagon policy restricting press access
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman sided with The New York Times, ruling that key parts of the credentialing restrictions imposed under Defense Secretary Hegseth are illegal.

stories.title: Jury finds Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors
stories.description: A San Francisco jury found that two of Musk's tweets during his 2022 Twitter acquisition constituted securities fraud, with damages likely in the billions.

stories.title: Iran offers to let Japanese ships pass through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi said Tehran is ready to facilitate transit for Japanese vessels if Tokyo coordinates with Iran, as negotiations continue.

stories.title: Iran fires ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia base
stories.description: Tehran launched two IRBMs at the joint U.S.-U.K. Indian Ocean base, marking its first known attempt to strike a target roughly 2,500 miles away.

stories.title: Hegseth faces backlash for invoking faith in Iran briefings
stories.description: The defense secretary's explicitly Christian rhetoric at Pentagon briefings has drawn sharp criticism as the U.S. enters its third week of war with Iran.

stories.title: Over 20 nations back Hormuz statement as Trump presses Japan to 'step up'
stories.description: A joint declaration condemning Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz grew from seven signatories to more than 20, though no country has committed warships.

stories.title: U.S. prosecutors open criminal probes into Colombia's President
stories.description: Federal offices in Manhattan and Brooklyn are examining whether Petro met with drug traffickers or received narcotics-linked campaign donations.

stories.title: Pentagon rushes Marines, warships to Middle East as Iran war widens
stories.description: The USS Boxer group and about 8,000 additional service members are headed to the region as the U.S. weighs further operations, including along Iran's coastline.

stories.title: Trump ally asked ICE to detain his ex in custody fight, NYT reports
stories.description: Paolo Zampolli contacted a senior ICE official to have his Brazilian ex-girlfriend detained and deported, aiding his bid for custody of their son.

stories.title: DOJ sues Harvard over alleged antisemitism on campus
stories.description: The lawsuit seeks billions in taxpayer funds back and accuses the university of being "deliberately indifferent" to harassment of Jewish and Israeli students.

stories.title: Amazon developing AI-powered smartphone a decade after Fire Phone flop
stories.description: The project, codenamed "Transformer," would center on Alexa and AI-driven interactions rather than competing on hardware specs with Apple and Samsung.

stories.title: Denmark planned to destroy Greenland runways if US invaded
stories.description: Danish broadcaster DR revealed that troops deployed to Greenland in January carried explosives to sabotage airstrips, with orders to fight if the U.S. attacked.

stories.title: US military readies Guantanamo Bay camp for potential Cuban exodus
stories.description: DeSantis warned of mass migration to Florida as the SOUTHCOM commander told Congress the military has plans to handle refugees at Guantanamo Bay.

stories.title: IRGC charges ships for 'safe' Hormuz passage
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps is routing vessels through a paid corridor near Larak Island, with at least one operator reportedly paying $2 million.

stories.title: Trump says Iran's military is 'gone,' rejects ceasefire talks
stories.description: As the U.S.-Israeli war enters its fourth week, the Pentagon says there is no timeline for ending the conflict.

stories.title: US bars Cuba, North Korea from Russia oil sanctions waiver
stories.description: OFAC amended its temporary Russian oil license after a tanker reportedly delivered diesel to fuel-starved Cuba using deceptive shipping practices.

stories.title: NASA's Artemis II moon rocket begins rollout to launch pad
stories.description: The SLS rocket started its 4-mile trek to Pad 39B early Friday, setting up a targeted April 1 crewed lunar launch.

stories.title: Trump privately concedes deportation push went too far, WSJ reports
stories.description: The president directed aides to rebrand the effort around arresting "bad guys" as polls show most Americans think enforcement has gone too far.

stories.title: Netanyahu says 'ground component' needed for Iran regime change
stories.description: The Israeli PM declared victory from the air but acknowledged airstrikes alone cannot topple Iran's government, hinting at ground options.

stories.title: Old-growth forests store far more carbon than logged ones, study finds
stories.description: A decade-long study published in Science reveals Sweden's primary forests hold 83% more carbon than managed woodlands, driven largely by overlooked soil reserves.

stories.title: Alibaba, Tencent shed $66 billion as AI plans fail to convince investors
stories.description: China's two largest tech companies lost a combined $66 billion in market value in roughly 24 hours after disappointing earnings and costly AI spending plans.

stories.title: EU leaders endorse tax cuts, ETS overhaul to fight energy price surge
stories.description: Von der Leyen pledged "temporary, tailored, and targeted" relief as the bloc grapples with soaring costs tied to the war in Iran.

stories.title: Live Nation CEO admits pay tied to 'solving' DOJ antitrust case
stories.description: Michael Rapino testified Thursday that about 90% of his compensation depends on profit targets and resolving the federal government's antitrust challenge.

stories.title: Sailor's Strava jog exposes French carrier's location amid Iran conflict
stories.description: Le Monde revealed that a naval officer's public fitness data pinpointed the Charles de Gaulle near Cyprus during its eastern Mediterranean deployment.

stories.title: Saudi officials warn oil could hit $180 if Iran war persists past April
stories.description: Internal Saudi projections show a phased price spiral from current levels to $180-plus per barrel by late April if Gulf supply disruptions continue.

stories.title: Tesla in talks to buy $2.9B in solar gear from Chinese firms
stories.description: The equipment would support Elon Musk's goal of building 100 gigawatts of U.S. solar manufacturing capacity by the end of 2028.

stories.title: S&P 500 heads for fourth straight weekly loss amid oil surge
stories.description: Oil prices spiked after Trump threatened to strike Iran's South Pars gas field, compounding Wednesday's Fed-driven selloff.

stories.title: OpenAI plans desktop 'superapp' merging ChatGPT, Codex, and browser
stories.description: The unified app aims to streamline the user experience by replacing multiple standalone AI tools with one desktop program.

stories.title: Blue Origin files for 51,600-satellite space data center network
stories.description: Project Sunrise would use orbital satellites to power AI computing, rivaling SpaceX's million-satellite proposal filed in January.

stories.title: Cuba braces for first Russian oil delivery in months amid energy crisis
stories.description: Two tankers carrying fuel are crossing the Atlantic after a U.S. blockade cut off foreign oil shipments, triggering blackouts lasting up to 29 hours.

stories.title: IAEA chief says Iran's nuclear program survives strikes
stories.description: Rafael Grossi told CBS that "a lot has survived" of Iran's nuclear capabilities and urged a return to diplomacy.

stories.title: Trump says he is 'not putting troops' in Iran amid uranium seizure debate
stories.description: The president's denial came as the Pentagon prepared options for a ground operation to secure enriched uranium that could yield up to 10 nuclear bombs.

stories.title: Study claims Chile's Monte Verde site is thousands of years younger than believed
stories.description: Research published in Science argues the landmark pre-Clovis site is only 4,200 to 8,200 years old, not 14,500, challenging theories on when humans first reached the Americas.

stories.title: Apple on track to top $1B in AI app fees in 2026
stories.description: The iPhone maker collected nearly $900 million from generative AI apps in 2025 — mostly from ChatGPT — without building its own AI models.

stories.title: DOJ, White House back continued criminal probe of Fed's Powell
stories.description: The move comes days after a federal judge quashed subpoenas and called the investigation a politically motivated effort to pressure rate cuts.

stories.title: US approves $16.5B in arms sales to Gulf allies amid Iran war
stories.description: The State Department notified Congress of deals with the UAE, Kuwait, and Jordan as the Pentagon seeks $200 billion more for the conflict.

stories.title: US new-home sales fell to lowest level since 2022 in January
stories.description: Sales plunged 17.6% from December to an annualized 587,000-unit pace, far worse than economists expected, as winter storms and rising headwinds weighed on buyers.

stories.title: Trump, Takaichi announce $40B nuclear reactor deal amid Iran tensions
stories.description: GE Vernova and Hitachi will build small modular reactors in Tennessee and Alabama as part of Japan's $550 billion U.S. investment fund.

stories.title: US F-35 makes emergency landing after suspected Iranian strike
stories.description: The incident marks the first reported case of Iran hitting a US aircraft since the conflict began on Feb. 28.

stories.title: Germany weighs windfall tax on oil firms as US Democrats push similar bill
stories.description: Lawmakers on both sides of the Atlantic are moving to tax oil company profits as crude prices surge amid the war on Iran.

stories.title: 36 states and cities sue Trump over climate rule rollbacks
stories.description: New York and California lead a coalition challenging the EPA's revocation of the endangerment finding and repeal of tailpipe emissions rules.

stories.title: 1 in 10 drop ACA coverage after subsidies expire, survey finds
stories.description: After enhanced premium tax credits expired, 80% of returning marketplace enrollees report higher costs and 55% are cutting back on food and basics.

stories.title: US jobless claims fall to lowest level since January
stories.description: Weekly claims dropped to 205,000, well below the 215,000 economists expected, though continuing claims rose as hiring remains sluggish.

stories.title: Scientists build mass spectrometer that traps a billion ions at once
stories.description: The new MultiQ-IT prototype processes ions in parallel rather than one at a time, a leap its creators compare to the shift from serial to parallel computing.

stories.title: Lewandowski demanded payments from contractors for DHS contracts
stories.description: Lawmakers allege Trump ally Corey Lewandowski acted as a "shadow chief of staff" and sought personal payments tied to government deals.

stories.title: Democrats storm out of Bondi's Epstein briefing, vow to enforce subpoena
stories.description: House Democrats left a closed-door session with the attorney general, saying she refused to commit to a sworn deposition on the Epstein files.

stories.title: US intelligence says China not planning Taiwan invasion in 2027
stories.description: The annual threat assessment found Beijing prefers unification without force and has no fixed timeline, softening earlier Pentagon warnings.

stories.title: BOJ holds rates steady, warns Middle East oil shock could fuel inflation
stories.description: The central bank kept its policy rate at 0.75% as surging energy prices from the Iran conflict complicate the outlook for further tightening.

stories.title: IAEA chief says status of Iran's new underground enrichment site is unknown
stories.description: Rafael Grossi said inspectors have never visited the Isfahan facility and cannot determine whether centrifuges have been installed.

stories.title: Tel Aviv researchers develop laser technique for longer-lasting battery anodes
stories.description: A single-step, room-temperature laser process produces silicon-graphene anodes that retain over 98% capacity after 2,000 charge cycles.

stories.title: Iran war energy crisis sparks global push for renewables
stories.description: UN climate chief says the Strait of Hormuz blockade proves fossil fuel dependence threatens national security, urging faster adoption of clean energy.

stories.title: Gabbard defers to Trump on whether Iran posed imminent threat before war
stories.description: At a Senate hearing, the DNI refused to say whether U.S. intelligence assessed an imminent Iranian threat, saying only the president can make that call.

stories.title: China pledges solar energy aid to Cuba after grid collapse
stories.description: Beijing vowed to accelerate renewable energy projects as Cuba's power grid failed, leaving nearly 11 million people without electricity for 29 hours.

stories.title: FBI is buying Americans' location data, director Patel tells Senate
stories.description: The practice resumed after the bureau's previous director said in 2023 that the agency was no longer actively purchasing such data.

stories.title: Vance vows gas prices will fall once Iran conflict ends
stories.description: As average gas prices hit $3.84 per gallon, the vice president called the surge "temporary" and promised a return to pre-war levels.

stories.title: U.S. wholesale prices jump more than expected in February
stories.description: The Producer Price Index rose 0.7% in February, more than double the forecast, as food and energy costs surged across the supply chain.

stories.title: DOJ antitrust chief calls political favoritism in Paramount-Warner deal 'ludicrous'
stories.description: Acting AAG Omeed Assefi told Reuters the roughly $110 billion merger review will not be fast-tracked due to the Ellison family's ties to Trump.

stories.title: Australian scientists build world's first quantum battery prototype
stories.description: CSIRO-led team created a device that charges, stores, and discharges energy using quantum mechanics — and charges faster as it grows.

stories.title: Fed holds rates steady, raises inflation forecast amid Iran war uncertainty
stories.description: Powell said the oil shock clouds the outlook, as officials raised their 2026 inflation projection to 2.7% and signaled at most one rate cut this year.

stories.title: FAA ends visual separation for helicopters near airports nationwide
stories.description: Controllers must now use radar to keep helicopters and planes apart, a sweeping change after the 2025 Potomac midair collision and recent close calls.

stories.title: Antarctic ice cores show ocean heat, not CO2, drove ancient climate shifts
stories.description: Two studies published Wednesday in Nature find that global ocean temperatures swung dramatically over 3 million years while greenhouse gas levels stayed broadly stable.

stories.title: Iran war squeezes US farmers as fertilizer costs soar ahead of planting
stories.description: Some farmers who haven't preordered fertilizer may not secure supplies for spring planting, the Farm Bureau Federation warns.

stories.title: Cuba vows 'impregnable resistance' after Trump threatens to 'take' island
stories.description: Diaz-Canel's defiance came as Cuba restored power after a 29-hour blackout tied to a U.S. oil blockade that has left the island in crisis.

stories.title: Kremlin condemns 'murder' of Iran's Ali Larijani in U.S.-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Spokesman Dmitry Peskov called the killing of Iran's de facto wartime leader an attack on a "sovereign and independent" nation's leadership.

stories.title: BP locks out 800 union workers at largest Midwest refinery
stories.description: The lockout at the Whiting, Indiana, facility takes effect March 19 after months of failed contract talks with United Steelworkers members.

stories.title: NATO allies in talks on how to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Secretary General Mark Rutte said allies are discussing the "best way" to restore passage through the vital waterway amid Trump's pressure for a naval coalition.

stories.title: China pledges emergency aid to Iran, Jordan, Lebanon and Iraq
stories.description: Beijing announced humanitarian assistance for four Middle Eastern nations as the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran enters its third week.

stories.title: Pentagon says 157 killed in drug boat strikes since September
stories.description: A senior defense official told Congress that 47 suspected narco-trafficking vessels were destroyed across 45 strikes in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific.

stories.title: Trump asks to delay Xi summit by a month over Iran war
stories.description: The president told reporters the ongoing conflict requires him to stay in Washington, pushing back a Beijing trip planned for late March.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with drones for 4th straight day
stories.description: Russia says it intercepted 206 drones overnight, including 40 aimed at Moscow, as strikes also hit military aviation plants deep inside Russian territory.

stories.title: Amazon CEO Jassy says AI could push AWS to $600B in annual sales by 2036
stories.description: The projection, made at an internal all-hands meeting, doubles Jassy's own prior estimate and implies a fivefold increase from AWS's 2025 revenue.

stories.title: UK adviser believed Iran deal was in reach before US-led attack
stories.description: Britain's national security adviser judged Tehran's nuclear offer could have averted war, as most allies reject Trump's call to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Moldova summons Russian envoy over oil spill from Ukraine strike
stories.description: Officials presented the ambassador with contaminated river water as cities remain without supply after a March 7 attack on a Ukrainian hydropower plant.

stories.title: Pentagon raises Golden Dome cost to $185B to speed space systems
stories.description: The $10 billion increase will accelerate satellite tracking, sensor and communications programs central to the homeland missile defense shield.

stories.title: Russia sharing satellite imagery, drone tech with Iran
stories.description: Moscow has expanded military cooperation to include satellite data and drone tactics refined during the Ukraine war, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Roberts calls personal attacks on judges 'dangerous' after Trump tirade
stories.description: The chief justice urged critics across the political spectrum to stop targeting judges personally, days after Trump called the Supreme Court "inept."

stories.title: UK and Ukraine sign drone defense pact during Zelenskyy's London visit
stories.description: Zelenskyy met with Starmer, Rutte, and King Charles, urging allies to keep focus on Ukraine as the Iran war shifts global attention toward the Middle East.

stories.title: UC Irvine, Tel Aviv team build ion pump with no moving parts
stories.description: The ratchet-based device removes salt and heavy metals from water using only a low-voltage electrical signal, with no chemical reactions required.

stories.title: Ukraine accepts EU offer to fund Druzhba pipeline repairs
stories.description: The deal aims to break Hungary's veto on a €90 billion aid package for Kyiv ahead of this week's EU summit.

stories.title: Microsoft unifies Copilot teams, shifts Suleyman to superintelligence role
stories.description: CEO Satya Nadella merged commercial and consumer Copilot divisions under new EVP Jacob Andreou while redirecting AI chief Mustafa Suleyman to frontier model development.

stories.title: Pentagon begins engineering work to replace Anthropic AI
stories.description: The Defense Department's top AI official said integration of a new large language model is already underway as the military moves past Anthropic's Claude.

stories.title: Democrats introduce BETS OFF Act to ban prediction market trades on government actions
stories.description: The bill is the fifth proposal this year targeting the fast-growing industry, spurred by suspicious trading ahead of U.S. strikes on Iran.

stories.title: Mastercard to buy stablecoin firm BVNK for up to $1.8B
stories.description: The deal is the largest stablecoin acquisition ever, eclipsing Stripe's $1.1 billion purchase of Bridge in 2025.

stories.title: US diesel tops $5 a gallon for first time since 2022
stories.description: The national average hit $5.04, up 34% since the Iran war began, as the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed to oil traffic.

stories.title: Russia vows solidarity with Cuba after Trump says he could 'take' the island
stories.description: Moscow condemned U.S. "interference" and pledged financial support to Havana after Trump told reporters he could do "anything I want" with Cuba.

stories.title: China's fuel export ban deepens Asia's energy crisis amid Iran war
stories.description: Beijing halted diesel, gasoline, and jet fuel exports as the Strait of Hormuz closure and Iran conflict drive prices sharply higher across the region.

stories.title: Top US Counterterrorism Official Joe Kent Resigns Over Iran War
stories.description: Kent accused Israeli officials of deploying "a misinformation campaign" that lured the U.S. into the conflict, now in its 17th day.

stories.title: AlphaFold Database adds millions of protein complex structures for the first time
stories.description: The expansion, a collaboration between EMBL-EBI, Google DeepMind, NVIDIA, and Seoul National University, targets proteins tied to human health and disease.

stories.title: Tropical peatland wildfires hit 2,000-year high, study finds
stories.description: Researchers analyzed charcoal records across four continents and linked the surge to drainage, deforestation and agricultural expansion in Southeast Asia.

stories.title: Israel says it killed Iran's security chief as Strait of Hormuz attacks continue
stories.description: A tanker was struck near a UAE port on Monday as the war enters its third week, with 500 vessels stuck or hit since hostilities began.

stories.title: U.S. Navy awards Gecko Robotics $71M to deploy AI and robots across Pacific Fleet
stories.description: The five-year contract, the Navy's largest robotics deal, will use wall-climbing robots to inspect 18 ships and help reach 80% fleet readiness by 2027.

stories.title: NASA taps Mars rover to watch the Sun ahead of Artemis II launch
stories.description: The Perseverance rover can spot sunspots two weeks early from Mars, giving mission teams advance warning as the April 1 launch window nears.

stories.title: Fire on USS Gerald R. Ford burned 30 hours, displaced 600 sailors
stories.description: A New York Times report reveals the full scope of the blaze aboard the carrier, now approaching 10 months at sea in the Red Sea.

stories.title: Taliban says Pakistani airstrike on Kabul hospital killed 400
stories.description: The strike hit a 2,000-bed drug rehabilitation facility Monday evening, marking one of the deadliest attacks in the weeks-long conflict between the neighbors.

stories.title: UCAR sues to block dismantling of NCAR weather research center
stories.description: The 129-university consortium filed a federal lawsuit alleging political retaliation and constitutional violations over the Trump administration's bid to break up the facility.

stories.title: U.S. transfers sacred Oak Flat land to Rio Tinto-BHP copper venture
stories.description: The Forest Service handed over 2,400 acres of Arizona's Tonto National Forest, clearing the way for one of North America's largest copper mines despite tribal opposition.

stories.title: Zambia balks at US deal tying HIV aid to mineral access
stories.description: The draft agreement would cut health funding while requiring mining concessions, data sharing, and domestic co-investment far exceeding terms offered to other African nations.

stories.title: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei names ex-IRGC chief as military adviser
stories.description: Mohsen Rezaei, a hardline former Revolutionary Guards commander, takes the role amid Iran's escalating conflict with the U.S. and Israel.

stories.title: Judge blocks RFK Jr.'s childhood vaccine schedule overhaul
stories.description: A federal judge said medical groups are likely to prove that Kennedy’s overhaul of the childhood vaccine schedule and his replacement of the CDC’s vaccine advisory panel violated federal law.

stories.title: Rubio orders diplomats to press allies for action against Iran
stories.description: The directive, in a private cable obtained by ABC News, sets a March 20 deadline as the U.S.-Iran war enters its third week.

stories.title: Ukraine hits oil depot 500 km from front in latest Krasnodar Krai strike
stories.description: The Labinsk attack caps a week of escalating drone strikes on southern Russia's energy network, including hits on the Tikhoretsk hub and Afipsky refinery.

stories.title: Vance publicly defends Trump's Iran war, dismisses rift reports
stories.description: The vice president called Trump a "smart president" and said he trusts him to avoid past mistakes, despite his own history of opposing foreign wars.

stories.title: EU sanctions 16 Iranian officials over January protest crackdown
stories.description: The measures target IRGC commanders, judiciary members, and a surveillance tech firm, bringing EU human rights sanctions on Iran to 263 individuals and 53 entities.

stories.title: Last detained Columbia protester freed after a year in ICE custody
stories.description: Leqaa Kordia was released Monday after the government declined to challenge a judge's third order for her release on $100,000 bond.

stories.title: Trump says he'll have the 'honor of taking Cuba'
stories.description: The president's most aggressive remarks yet on Cuba came during a White House event, as both governments have confirmed ongoing diplomatic talks.

stories.title: EU sanctions 13 over Bucha massacre and Kremlin disinformation
stories.description: The measures target nine Russian military officers linked to the 2022 killings and four individuals accused of spreading pro-Kremlin propaganda.

stories.title: Drone strike sparks fire at Abu Dhabi's Shah oil field
stories.description: The attack is the latest in a series of Iranian strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure since the U.S.-Israel war with Iran began in early March.

stories.title: Rubio designates Iranian American prisoner as wrongfully detained
stories.description: Kamran Hekmati, jailed for visiting Israel for his son's bar mitzvah over a decade ago, is now the second American given the formal designation.

stories.title: Trump discloses Rep. Dunn's terminal heart diagnosis at White House event
stories.description: The president revealed the Florida Republican would have been "dead by June" without emergency surgery, catching Speaker Johnson off guard.

stories.title: DOJ proposes rule to help states fast-track executions
stories.description: The proposed change would ease certification requirements under a 1996 law that no state has ever successfully used to expedite federal death penalty reviews.

stories.title: US and Iran reestablish direct contact amid ongoing conflict
stories.description: Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi exchanged text messages, the first known direct communication since the war began Feb. 28.

stories.title: All five DNA and RNA building blocks found in asteroid Ryugu samples
stories.description: A Nature Astronomy study reports the complete set of nucleobases in Ryugu material, making it the second asteroid confirmed to carry life's genetic ingredients.

stories.title: Meta finalizes $27B AI deal with Nebius amid plans to cut 16,000 jobs
stories.description: The five-year infrastructure pact, Meta's largest cloud contract, comes as the company reportedly prepares to lay off up to 20% of its workforce.

stories.title: Cuba to let expatriates invest in and own businesses on the island
stories.description: Deputy Prime Minister Fraga announced the reforms in an NBC News interview, as the island faces its worst energy and economic crisis in decades.

stories.title: Susie Wiles diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer
stories.description: Trump announced the diagnosis on Truth Social, saying his chief of staff has an "excellent" prognosis and will keep working during treatment.

top.title: Iran vows to destroy regional infrastructure if Trump strikes its power plants
top.description: Tehran warned that energy and desalination facilities would become "legitimate targets" after Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Senate advances Mullin's nomination for DHS secretary
top.description: The 54-37 vote clears a key procedural hurdle, setting up a final confirmation vote as early as Monday.

top.title: Zelenskyy demands tighter Russia sanctions as Florida peace talks enter day two
top.description: Ukraine's president cited 1,550 drone strikes in one week, saying eased oil sanctions fund Russia's war as bilateral talks with the U.S. continue.

top.title: Trump draws bipartisan rebuke for celebrating Mueller's death
top.description: Robert Mueller, the former FBI director and special counsel in the Trump-Russia probe, died Friday at 81. Trump said he was "glad" on Truth Social.

top.title: Houthis could enter Iran war as soon as Monday
top.description: Saudi Arabia is working to deter the group as it reinforces positions near a key Red Sea port, threatening a second maritime front.

top.title: Bessent says US has 'plenty of money' for Iran war
top.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent ruled out tax increases to fund the conflict and defended the Pentagon's request for $200 billion in supplemental funding.

top.title: Trump threatens to deploy ICE agents to airports Monday amid DHS shutdown
top.description: The president said immigration officers would provide security and arrest undocumented immigrants as TSA staffing shortages worsen nationwide.

top.title: IAEA says no radiation leaks after strikes near nuclear sites in Iran, Israel
top.description: Tit-for-tat attacks hit near Natanz and Dimona on day 23 of the war, with IAEA chief Grossi urging "maximum military restraint."

top.title: Senate blocks transgender sports ban amendment to voting bill
top.description: The 49-41 party-line vote rejected a GOP push to attach the ban to the SAVE America Act during a rare weekend session.

top.title: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in March as US oil blockade tightens
top.description: The grid collapse left millions without power as aging infrastructure buckles under fuel shortages caused by the Trump administration's economic pressure campaign.

top.title: US envoy calls Miami talks with Ukraine 'constructive' amid deadly attacks
top.description: Special Envoy Steve Witkoff said discussions with Ukrainian officials focused on narrowing gaps toward a peace deal as both sides traded fire Saturday.

top.title: U.S. intensifies strikes to reopen Strait of Hormuz as Iran war roils global energy markets
top.description: Oil prices have roughly doubled and gas costs jumped since Iran blockaded the strait three weeks ago, stranding thousands of vessels.

top.title: Rising gas prices are wiping out Trump tax refunds, economists warn
top.description: Stanford economists found the typical household will spend $740 more on gas this year, nearly matching the $748 boost in tax refunds from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act.

top.title: G7 demands Iran halt attacks, vows to protect energy supplies
top.description: The joint statement condemned strikes across eight Middle Eastern nations and backed the right of targeted countries to defend themselves.

top.title: US says it struck 8,000 Iranian targets in three weeks of war
top.description: CENTCOM says 130 Iranian vessels have been destroyed, calling it the largest elimination of a navy since World War II.

top.title: Robert Mueller, former FBI director and special counsel, dies at 81
top.description: Mueller reshaped the FBI after 9/11 and led the investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 election during Trump's first term.

top.title: US quietly lifts sanctions on Russian nationals in fourth wave this month
top.description: The Treasury Department removed two Russian citizens and several other entities from its sanctions list on March 20, offering no explanation.

top.title: UK expands US base access to strike Iran's Strait of Hormuz missile sites
top.description: Starmer had initially limited US use of British bases to defensive measures but broadened the terms Friday to include protecting commercial shipping.

top.title: Indian, Asian refiners move to buy Iranian oil after US sanctions waiver
top.description: The 30-day exemption covers roughly 170 million barrels of Iranian crude already at sea, the third such waiver in two weeks.

top.title: Gold suffers worst weekly drop since 1983 as war stokes rate fears
top.description: The metal shed roughly 10% in a week, defying its safe-haven reputation as the Iran conflict rewired inflation and interest rate expectations.

top.title: Democrats conduct informal vote counts on ousting Schumer
top.description: Sen. Chris Murphy revealed that Democratic lawmakers have tallied votes on removing Schumer as minority leader, though he said Schumer retains enough support to stay.

top.title: Trump lifts sanctions on Iranian oil at sea to ease surging prices
top.description: The Treasury Department issued a general license Friday permitting global sale of roughly 140 million barrels of Iranian crude through April 19.

top.title: Switzerland halts new arms exports to US over Iran war
top.description: Bern cited its neutrality obligations, saying no new weapons licenses have been issued to Washington since the conflict began Feb. 28.

top.title: Judge blocks Pentagon policy restricting press access
top.description: U.S. District Judge Paul Friedman sided with The New York Times, ruling that key parts of the credentialing restrictions imposed under Defense Secretary Hegseth are illegal.

top.title: Jury finds Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors
top.description: A San Francisco jury found that two of Musk's tweets during his 2022 Twitter acquisition constituted securities fraud, with damages likely in the billions.

top.title: Iran offers to let Japanese ships pass through Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi said Tehran is ready to facilitate transit for Japanese vessels if Tokyo coordinates with Iran, as negotiations continue.

top.title: Iran fires ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia base
top.description: Tehran launched two IRBMs at the joint U.S.-U.K. Indian Ocean base, marking its first known attempt to strike a target roughly 2,500 miles away.

top.title: Hegseth faces backlash for invoking faith in Iran briefings
top.description: The defense secretary's explicitly Christian rhetoric at Pentagon briefings has drawn sharp criticism as the U.S. enters its third week of war with Iran.

top.title: Over 20 nations back Hormuz statement as Trump presses Japan to 'step up'
top.description: A joint declaration condemning Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz grew from seven signatories to more than 20, though no country has committed warships.

top.title: U.S. prosecutors open criminal probes into Colombia's President
top.description: Federal offices in Manhattan and Brooklyn are examining whether Petro met with drug traffickers or received narcotics-linked campaign donations.

top.title: Pentagon rushes Marines, warships to Middle East as Iran war widens
top.description: The USS Boxer group and about 8,000 additional service members are headed to the region as the U.S. weighs further operations, including along Iran's coastline.

top.title: Trump ally asked ICE to detain his ex in custody fight, NYT reports
top.description: Paolo Zampolli contacted a senior ICE official to have his Brazilian ex-girlfriend detained and deported, aiding his bid for custody of their son.

top.title: DOJ sues Harvard over alleged antisemitism on campus
top.description: The lawsuit seeks billions in taxpayer funds back and accuses the university of being "deliberately indifferent" to harassment of Jewish and Israeli students.

top.title: Amazon developing AI-powered smartphone a decade after Fire Phone flop
top.description: The project, codenamed "Transformer," would center on Alexa and AI-driven interactions rather than competing on hardware specs with Apple and Samsung.

top.title: Denmark planned to destroy Greenland runways if US invaded
top.description: Danish broadcaster DR revealed that troops deployed to Greenland in January carried explosives to sabotage airstrips, with orders to fight if the U.S. attacked.

top.title: US military readies Guantanamo Bay camp for potential Cuban exodus
top.description: DeSantis warned of mass migration to Florida as the SOUTHCOM commander told Congress the military has plans to handle refugees at Guantanamo Bay.

top.title: IRGC charges ships for 'safe' Hormuz passage
top.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps is routing vessels through a paid corridor near Larak Island, with at least one operator reportedly paying $2 million.

top.title: Trump says Iran's military is 'gone,' rejects ceasefire talks
top.description: As the U.S.-Israeli war enters its fourth week, the Pentagon says there is no timeline for ending the conflict.

top.title: US bars Cuba, North Korea from Russia oil sanctions waiver
top.description: OFAC amended its temporary Russian oil license after a tanker reportedly delivered diesel to fuel-starved Cuba using deceptive shipping practices.

top.title: NASA's Artemis II moon rocket begins rollout to launch pad
top.description: The SLS rocket started its 4-mile trek to Pad 39B early Friday, setting up a targeted April 1 crewed lunar launch.

top.title: Trump privately concedes deportation push went too far, WSJ reports
top.description: The president directed aides to rebrand the effort around arresting "bad guys" as polls show most Americans think enforcement has gone too far.

top.title: Netanyahu says 'ground component' needed for Iran regime change
top.description: The Israeli PM declared victory from the air but acknowledged airstrikes alone cannot topple Iran's government, hinting at ground options.

top.title: Old-growth forests store far more carbon than logged ones, study finds
top.description: A decade-long study published in Science reveals Sweden's primary forests hold 83% more carbon than managed woodlands, driven largely by overlooked soil reserves.

top.title: Alibaba, Tencent shed $66 billion as AI plans fail to convince investors
top.description: China's two largest tech companies lost a combined $66 billion in market value in roughly 24 hours after disappointing earnings and costly AI spending plans.

top.title: EU leaders endorse tax cuts, ETS overhaul to fight energy price surge
top.description: Von der Leyen pledged "temporary, tailored, and targeted" relief as the bloc grapples with soaring costs tied to the war in Iran.

top.title: Live Nation CEO admits pay tied to 'solving' DOJ antitrust case
top.description: Michael Rapino testified Thursday that about 90% of his compensation depends on profit targets and resolving the federal government's antitrust challenge.

top.title: Sailor's Strava jog exposes French carrier's location amid Iran conflict
top.description: Le Monde revealed that a naval officer's public fitness data pinpointed the Charles de Gaulle near Cyprus during its eastern Mediterranean deployment.

top.title: Saudi officials warn oil could hit $180 if Iran war persists past April
top.description: Internal Saudi projections show a phased price spiral from current levels to $180-plus per barrel by late April if Gulf supply disruptions continue.

top.title: Tesla in talks to buy $2.9B in solar gear from Chinese firms
top.description: The equipment would support Elon Musk's goal of building 100 gigawatts of U.S. solar manufacturing capacity by the end of 2028.

top.title: S&P 500 heads for fourth straight weekly loss amid oil surge
top.description: Oil prices spiked after Trump threatened to strike Iran's South Pars gas field, compounding Wednesday's Fed-driven selloff.

top.title: OpenAI plans desktop 'superapp' merging ChatGPT, Codex, and browser
top.description: The unified app aims to streamline the user experience by replacing multiple standalone AI tools with one desktop program.

top.title: Blue Origin files for 51,600-satellite space data center network
top.description: Project Sunrise would use orbital satellites to power AI computing, rivaling SpaceX's million-satellite proposal filed in January.

top.title: Cuba braces for first Russian oil delivery in months amid energy crisis
top.description: Two tankers carrying fuel are crossing the Atlantic after a U.S. blockade cut off foreign oil shipments, triggering blackouts lasting up to 29 hours.

top.title: IAEA chief says Iran's nuclear program survives strikes
top.description: Rafael Grossi told CBS that "a lot has survived" of Iran's nuclear capabilities and urged a return to diplomacy.

top.title: Trump says he is 'not putting troops' in Iran amid uranium seizure debate
top.description: The president's denial came as the Pentagon prepared options for a ground operation to secure enriched uranium that could yield up to 10 nuclear bombs.

top.title: Study claims Chile's Monte Verde site is thousands of years younger than believed
top.description: Research published in Science argues the landmark pre-Clovis site is only 4,200 to 8,200 years old, not 14,500, challenging theories on when humans first reached the Americas.

top.title: Apple on track to top $1B in AI app fees in 2026
top.description: The iPhone maker collected nearly $900 million from generative AI apps in 2025 — mostly from ChatGPT — without building its own AI models.

top.title: DOJ, White House back continued criminal probe of Fed's Powell
top.description: The move comes days after a federal judge quashed subpoenas and called the investigation a politically motivated effort to pressure rate cuts.

top.title: US approves $16.5B in arms sales to Gulf allies amid Iran war
top.description: The State Department notified Congress of deals with the UAE, Kuwait, and Jordan as the Pentagon seeks $200 billion more for the conflict.

top.title: US new-home sales fell to lowest level since 2022 in January
top.description: Sales plunged 17.6% from December to an annualized 587,000-unit pace, far worse than economists expected, as winter storms and rising headwinds weighed on buyers.

top.title: Trump, Takaichi announce $40B nuclear reactor deal amid Iran tensions
top.description: GE Vernova and Hitachi will build small modular reactors in Tennessee and Alabama as part of Japan's $550 billion U.S. investment fund.

top.title: US F-35 makes emergency landing after suspected Iranian strike
top.description: The incident marks the first reported case of Iran hitting a US aircraft since the conflict began on Feb. 28.

top.title: Germany weighs windfall tax on oil firms as US Democrats push similar bill
top.description: Lawmakers on both sides of the Atlantic are moving to tax oil company profits as crude prices surge amid the war on Iran.

top.title: 36 states and cities sue Trump over climate rule rollbacks
top.description: New York and California lead a coalition challenging the EPA's revocation of the endangerment finding and repeal of tailpipe emissions rules.

top.title: 1 in 10 drop ACA coverage after subsidies expire, survey finds
top.description: After enhanced premium tax credits expired, 80% of returning marketplace enrollees report higher costs and 55% are cutting back on food and basics.

top.title: US jobless claims fall to lowest level since January
top.description: Weekly claims dropped to 205,000, well below the 215,000 economists expected, though continuing claims rose as hiring remains sluggish.

top.title: Scientists build mass spectrometer that traps a billion ions at once
top.description: The new MultiQ-IT prototype processes ions in parallel rather than one at a time, a leap its creators compare to the shift from serial to parallel computing.

top.title: Lewandowski demanded payments from contractors for DHS contracts
top.description: Lawmakers allege Trump ally Corey Lewandowski acted as a "shadow chief of staff" and sought personal payments tied to government deals.

top.title: Democrats storm out of Bondi's Epstein briefing, vow to enforce subpoena
top.description: House Democrats left a closed-door session with the attorney general, saying she refused to commit to a sworn deposition on the Epstein files.

top.title: US intelligence says China not planning Taiwan invasion in 2027
top.description: The annual threat assessment found Beijing prefers unification without force and has no fixed timeline, softening earlier Pentagon warnings.

top.title: BOJ holds rates steady, warns Middle East oil shock could fuel inflation
top.description: The central bank kept its policy rate at 0.75% as surging energy prices from the Iran conflict complicate the outlook for further tightening.

top.title: IAEA chief says status of Iran's new underground enrichment site is unknown
top.description: Rafael Grossi said inspectors have never visited the Isfahan facility and cannot determine whether centrifuges have been installed.

top.title: Tel Aviv researchers develop laser technique for longer-lasting battery anodes
top.description: A single-step, room-temperature laser process produces silicon-graphene anodes that retain over 98% capacity after 2,000 charge cycles.

top.title: Iran war energy crisis sparks global push for renewables
top.description: UN climate chief says the Strait of Hormuz blockade proves fossil fuel dependence threatens national security, urging faster adoption of clean energy.

top.title: Gabbard defers to Trump on whether Iran posed imminent threat before war
top.description: At a Senate hearing, the DNI refused to say whether U.S. intelligence assessed an imminent Iranian threat, saying only the president can make that call.

top.title: China pledges solar energy aid to Cuba after grid collapse
top.description: Beijing vowed to accelerate renewable energy projects as Cuba's power grid failed, leaving nearly 11 million people without electricity for 29 hours.

top.title: FBI is buying Americans' location data, director Patel tells Senate
top.description: The practice resumed after the bureau's previous director said in 2023 that the agency was no longer actively purchasing such data.

top.title: Vance vows gas prices will fall once Iran conflict ends
top.description: As average gas prices hit $3.84 per gallon, the vice president called the surge "temporary" and promised a return to pre-war levels.

top.title: U.S. wholesale prices jump more than expected in February
top.description: The Producer Price Index rose 0.7% in February, more than double the forecast, as food and energy costs surged across the supply chain.

top.title: DOJ antitrust chief calls political favoritism in Paramount-Warner deal 'ludicrous'
top.description: Acting AAG Omeed Assefi told Reuters the roughly $110 billion merger review will not be fast-tracked due to the Ellison family's ties to Trump.

top.title: Australian scientists build world's first quantum battery prototype
top.description: CSIRO-led team created a device that charges, stores, and discharges energy using quantum mechanics — and charges faster as it grows.

top.title: Fed holds rates steady, raises inflation forecast amid Iran war uncertainty
top.description: Powell said the oil shock clouds the outlook, as officials raised their 2026 inflation projection to 2.7% and signaled at most one rate cut this year.

top.title: FAA ends visual separation for helicopters near airports nationwide
top.description: Controllers must now use radar to keep helicopters and planes apart, a sweeping change after the 2025 Potomac midair collision and recent close calls.

top.title: Antarctic ice cores show ocean heat, not CO2, drove ancient climate shifts
top.description: Two studies published Wednesday in Nature find that global ocean temperatures swung dramatically over 3 million years while greenhouse gas levels stayed broadly stable.

top.title: Iran war squeezes US farmers as fertilizer costs soar ahead of planting
top.description: Some farmers who haven't preordered fertilizer may not secure supplies for spring planting, the Farm Bureau Federation warns.

top.title: Cuba vows 'impregnable resistance' after Trump threatens to 'take' island
top.description: Diaz-Canel's defiance came as Cuba restored power after a 29-hour blackout tied to a U.S. oil blockade that has left the island in crisis.

top.title: Kremlin condemns 'murder' of Iran's Ali Larijani in U.S.-Israeli strikes
top.description: Spokesman Dmitry Peskov called the killing of Iran's de facto wartime leader an attack on a "sovereign and independent" nation's leadership.

top.title: BP locks out 800 union workers at largest Midwest refinery
top.description: The lockout at the Whiting, Indiana, facility takes effect March 19 after months of failed contract talks with United Steelworkers members.

top.title: NATO allies in talks on how to reopen Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Secretary General Mark Rutte said allies are discussing the "best way" to restore passage through the vital waterway amid Trump's pressure for a naval coalition.

top.title: China pledges emergency aid to Iran, Jordan, Lebanon and Iraq
top.description: Beijing announced humanitarian assistance for four Middle Eastern nations as the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran enters its third week.

top.title: Pentagon says 157 killed in drug boat strikes since September
top.description: A senior defense official told Congress that 47 suspected narco-trafficking vessels were destroyed across 45 strikes in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific.

top.title: Trump asks to delay Xi summit by a month over Iran war
top.description: The president told reporters the ongoing conflict requires him to stay in Washington, pushing back a Beijing trip planned for late March.

top.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with drones for 4th straight day
top.description: Russia says it intercepted 206 drones overnight, including 40 aimed at Moscow, as strikes also hit military aviation plants deep inside Russian territory.

top.title: Amazon CEO Jassy says AI could push AWS to $600B in annual sales by 2036
top.description: The projection, made at an internal all-hands meeting, doubles Jassy's own prior estimate and implies a fivefold increase from AWS's 2025 revenue.

top.title: UK adviser believed Iran deal was in reach before US-led attack
top.description: Britain's national security adviser judged Tehran's nuclear offer could have averted war, as most allies reject Trump's call to secure the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Moldova summons Russian envoy over oil spill from Ukraine strike
top.description: Officials presented the ambassador with contaminated river water as cities remain without supply after a March 7 attack on a Ukrainian hydropower plant.

top.title: Pentagon raises Golden Dome cost to $185B to speed space systems
top.description: The $10 billion increase will accelerate satellite tracking, sensor and communications programs central to the homeland missile defense shield.

top.title: Russia sharing satellite imagery, drone tech with Iran
top.description: Moscow has expanded military cooperation to include satellite data and drone tactics refined during the Ukraine war, according to The Wall Street Journal.

top.title: Roberts calls personal attacks on judges 'dangerous' after Trump tirade
top.description: The chief justice urged critics across the political spectrum to stop targeting judges personally, days after Trump called the Supreme Court "inept."

top.title: UK and Ukraine sign drone defense pact during Zelenskyy's London visit
top.description: Zelenskyy met with Starmer, Rutte, and King Charles, urging allies to keep focus on Ukraine as the Iran war shifts global attention toward the Middle East.

top.title: UC Irvine, Tel Aviv team build ion pump with no moving parts
top.description: The ratchet-based device removes salt and heavy metals from water using only a low-voltage electrical signal, with no chemical reactions required.

top.title: Ukraine accepts EU offer to fund Druzhba pipeline repairs
top.description: The deal aims to break Hungary's veto on a €90 billion aid package for Kyiv ahead of this week's EU summit.

top.title: Microsoft unifies Copilot teams, shifts Suleyman to superintelligence role
top.description: CEO Satya Nadella merged commercial and consumer Copilot divisions under new EVP Jacob Andreou while redirecting AI chief Mustafa Suleyman to frontier model development.

top.title: Pentagon begins engineering work to replace Anthropic AI
top.description: The Defense Department's top AI official said integration of a new large language model is already underway as the military moves past Anthropic's Claude.

top.title: Democrats introduce BETS OFF Act to ban prediction market trades on government actions
top.description: The bill is the fifth proposal this year targeting the fast-growing industry, spurred by suspicious trading ahead of U.S. strikes on Iran.

top.title: Mastercard to buy stablecoin firm BVNK for up to $1.8B
top.description: The deal is the largest stablecoin acquisition ever, eclipsing Stripe's $1.1 billion purchase of Bridge in 2025.

top.title: US diesel tops $5 a gallon for first time since 2022
top.description: The national average hit $5.04, up 34% since the Iran war began, as the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed to oil traffic.

top.title: Russia vows solidarity with Cuba after Trump says he could 'take' the island
top.description: Moscow condemned U.S. "interference" and pledged financial support to Havana after Trump told reporters he could do "anything I want" with Cuba.

top.title: China's fuel export ban deepens Asia's energy crisis amid Iran war
top.description: Beijing halted diesel, gasoline, and jet fuel exports as the Strait of Hormuz closure and Iran conflict drive prices sharply higher across the region.

top.title: Top US Counterterrorism Official Joe Kent Resigns Over Iran War
top.description: Kent accused Israeli officials of deploying "a misinformation campaign" that lured the U.S. into the conflict, now in its 17th day.

top.title: AlphaFold Database adds millions of protein complex structures for the first time
top.description: The expansion, a collaboration between EMBL-EBI, Google DeepMind, NVIDIA, and Seoul National University, targets proteins tied to human health and disease.

top.title: Tropical peatland wildfires hit 2,000-year high, study finds
top.description: Researchers analyzed charcoal records across four continents and linked the surge to drainage, deforestation and agricultural expansion in Southeast Asia.

top.title: Israel says it killed Iran's security chief as Strait of Hormuz attacks continue
top.description: A tanker was struck near a UAE port on Monday as the war enters its third week, with 500 vessels stuck or hit since hostilities began.

top.title: U.S. Navy awards Gecko Robotics $71M to deploy AI and robots across Pacific Fleet
top.description: The five-year contract, the Navy's largest robotics deal, will use wall-climbing robots to inspect 18 ships and help reach 80% fleet readiness by 2027.

top.title: NASA taps Mars rover to watch the Sun ahead of Artemis II launch
top.description: The Perseverance rover can spot sunspots two weeks early from Mars, giving mission teams advance warning as the April 1 launch window nears.

top.title: Fire on USS Gerald R. Ford burned 30 hours, displaced 600 sailors
top.description: A New York Times report reveals the full scope of the blaze aboard the carrier, now approaching 10 months at sea in the Red Sea.

top.title: Taliban says Pakistani airstrike on Kabul hospital killed 400
top.description: The strike hit a 2,000-bed drug rehabilitation facility Monday evening, marking one of the deadliest attacks in the weeks-long conflict between the neighbors.

top.title: UCAR sues to block dismantling of NCAR weather research center
top.description: The 129-university consortium filed a federal lawsuit alleging political retaliation and constitutional violations over the Trump administration's bid to break up the facility.

top.title: U.S. transfers sacred Oak Flat land to Rio Tinto-BHP copper venture
top.description: The Forest Service handed over 2,400 acres of Arizona's Tonto National Forest, clearing the way for one of North America's largest copper mines despite tribal opposition.

top.title: Zambia balks at US deal tying HIV aid to mineral access
top.description: The draft agreement would cut health funding while requiring mining concessions, data sharing, and domestic co-investment far exceeding terms offered to other African nations.

top.title: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei names ex-IRGC chief as military adviser
top.description: Mohsen Rezaei, a hardline former Revolutionary Guards commander, takes the role amid Iran's escalating conflict with the U.S. and Israel.

top.title: Judge blocks RFK Jr.'s childhood vaccine schedule overhaul
top.description: A federal judge said medical groups are likely to prove that Kennedy’s overhaul of the childhood vaccine schedule and his replacement of the CDC’s vaccine advisory panel violated federal law.

top.title: Rubio orders diplomats to press allies for action against Iran
top.description: The directive, in a private cable obtained by ABC News, sets a March 20 deadline as the U.S.-Iran war enters its third week.

top.title: Ukraine hits oil depot 500 km from front in latest Krasnodar Krai strike
top.description: The Labinsk attack caps a week of escalating drone strikes on southern Russia's energy network, including hits on the Tikhoretsk hub and Afipsky refinery.

top.title: Vance publicly defends Trump's Iran war, dismisses rift reports
top.description: The vice president called Trump a "smart president" and said he trusts him to avoid past mistakes, despite his own history of opposing foreign wars.

top.title: EU sanctions 16 Iranian officials over January protest crackdown
top.description: The measures target IRGC commanders, judiciary members, and a surveillance tech firm, bringing EU human rights sanctions on Iran to 263 individuals and 53 entities.

top.title: Last detained Columbia protester freed after a year in ICE custody
top.description: Leqaa Kordia was released Monday after the government declined to challenge a judge's third order for her release on $100,000 bond.

top.title: Trump says he'll have the 'honor of taking Cuba'
top.description: The president's most aggressive remarks yet on Cuba came during a White House event, as both governments have confirmed ongoing diplomatic talks.

top.title: EU sanctions 13 over Bucha massacre and Kremlin disinformation
top.description: The measures target nine Russian military officers linked to the 2022 killings and four individuals accused of spreading pro-Kremlin propaganda.

top.title: Drone strike sparks fire at Abu Dhabi's Shah oil field
top.description: The attack is the latest in a series of Iranian strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure since the U.S.-Israel war with Iran began in early March.

top.title: Rubio designates Iranian American prisoner as wrongfully detained
top.description: Kamran Hekmati, jailed for visiting Israel for his son's bar mitzvah over a decade ago, is now the second American given the formal designation.

top.title: Trump discloses Rep. Dunn's terminal heart diagnosis at White House event
top.description: The president revealed the Florida Republican would have been "dead by June" without emergency surgery, catching Speaker Johnson off guard.

top.title: DOJ proposes rule to help states fast-track executions
top.description: The proposed change would ease certification requirements under a 1996 law that no state has ever successfully used to expedite federal death penalty reviews.

top.title: US and Iran reestablish direct contact amid ongoing conflict
top.description: Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi exchanged text messages, the first known direct communication since the war began Feb. 28.

top.title: All five DNA and RNA building blocks found in asteroid Ryugu samples
top.description: A Nature Astronomy study reports the complete set of nucleobases in Ryugu material, making it the second asteroid confirmed to carry life's genetic ingredients.

top.title: Meta finalizes $27B AI deal with Nebius amid plans to cut 16,000 jobs
top.description: The five-year infrastructure pact, Meta's largest cloud contract, comes as the company reportedly prepares to lay off up to 20% of its workforce.

top.title: Cuba to let expatriates invest in and own businesses on the island
top.description: Deputy Prime Minister Fraga announced the reforms in an NBC News interview, as the island faces its worst energy and economic crisis in decades.

top.title: Susie Wiles diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer
top.description: Trump announced the diagnosis on Truth Social, saying his chief of staff has an "excellent" prognosis and will keep working during treatment.
